title: Project Manager – Rail Infrastructure (Permanent)
description_raw:
Network Rail are recruiting for a Permanent Project Manager to join their Infrastructure Projects (IP) North team based in Leeds. This is a staff appointment with full Network Rail benefits.

You will be responsible for delivering track and civils renewals projects within the Yorkshire & North East route. The role forms part of the Control Period 7 (CP7) investment programme and involves managing projects from GRIP Stage 3/4 through to handback and final account.

Key responsibilities:
- Own the project cost plan from estimate through to final account (£2–15m project range)
- Prepare and present business cases at project approval gates
- Lead multi-discipline project teams through design, procurement and delivery
- Manage third-party interfaces (Local Authorities, utilities, other project teams)
- Comply with NR Project Control Framework (PCF) at all GRIP stages

Candidate requirements:
- Minimum 5 years’ rail project management experience
- PRINCE2 Practitioner or APM PMQ
- Understanding of NR engineering standards and GRIP process
- Full UK driving licence

Salary: £65,000–£75,000 per annum + generous pension (defined benefit) + flexible working
Contract type: Permanent (staff)
Location: Leeds (hybrid working available: 3 days on site / 2 days home office)
Benefits: 28 days holiday, free travel on Network Rail-sponsored trains
